briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting risks in the BaaS model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly unconventional credit card products with novel rewards, structures, and underwriting models.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling deeper ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[How the Middle East war is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/iran-war-mortgage-rates-canada-9.7152182?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising geopolitical tensions stemming from the Iran conflict are putting upward pressure on Canadian mortgage rates, affecting borrowers nationwide.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government declared screen scraping a security risk and committed to establishing an open banking framework, a policy journey still unfolding today.

- **[Why many of Canada's tech centaurs are in no rush to go public](https://betakit.com/why-many-of-canadas-tech-centaurs-are-in-no-rush-to-go-public/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's largest private tech companies are opting to stay private longer, reflecting cautious sentiment around IPO markets amid global economic uncertainty.

- **[Which consumer services are adding a fuel surcharge (or not) as oil prices rise?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/fuel-surcharge-list-roundup-9.7144830?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — As oil prices surge due to Middle East conflict, Canadian consumer-facing companies are rolling out fuel surcharges, adding cost pressure on households and payments.

- **[Amazon adding fuel surcharge for Canadian, U.S. sellers starting April 17](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/amazon-fuel-surcharge-canada-april-17-9.7151984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Amazon will begin charging Canadian and U.S. marketplace sellers a fuel surcharge starting April 17, increasing costs for e-commerce businesses reliant on the platform.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative look at the darker side of fintech's promises, raising important questions about consumer protection, financial inclusion, and the risks of under-regulated innovation — themes directly relevant to building a responsible fintech ecosystem in Atlantic Canada.

- **[New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and get refunds](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cvg0v36ek2go?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — The UK government is introducing consumer-friendly legislation requiring one-click subscription cancellations, a regulatory trend worth watching for Canadian fintech and embedded finance players building subscription-based products.
